caption: This sequence of frames displays a presentation slide for a course in "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING," specifically focusing on "Tutorial – 2" regarding "Ideal Work Requirement." The slide features a thermodynamic formula, $W_{i} = T_{i}(s_{i} - s_{f}) - (h_{i} - h_{f})$, and a data table listing values for pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s) at states 1, 2, and f. A corresponding T-s diagram shows a process path with a section labeled "p=const." At the bottom, a calculation is being performed: $W_{i} = 300(4.4 \pm 0.42) - (462 - 29)$. As the sequence progresses, the equation updates to reflect a subtraction sign, $W_{i} = 300(4.4 - 0.42) - (462 - 29)$, and finally concludes with the calculated result, $W_{i} = 300(4.4 - 0.42) - (462 - 29) = 761 \text{ J/g}$. The footer attributes the material to "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay."
